TextENGNew: "Welcome back!!"
changeReason: "I'm back" changed to "Welcome back" -- the Japanese お疲れさまです is said by the greeter to welcome someone back, not by the person arriving.

TextENGNew: "How did it go? Find any leads?"
...The officers shook their heads with obvious disappointment.
Exhaustion was written plainly across every face.
It was only natural.@kNone of them had slept a wink since the day of Watanagashi.
"Chief, please let the younger ones take naps in shifts.@k@vS20/11/440700162.We've got a long haul ahead of us.@k@vS20/11/440700163.Let's not push too hard -- rest up and take it steady."
"I keep telling them to rest in shifts, but nobody wants to be the first to lie down.@k@vS20/00/442700021....Could you tell them, Ooishi-san?"
"Oh dear. You really don't need to force yourselves, you know?@k@vS20/11/440700165.Komiyama-kun.@k@vS20/11/440700166.Please arrange for everyone to rest in shifts, starting with the youngest."
"Komiyama-san. Please see to it."
With the order now repeated by the chief, Komiyama-kun rose from his seat.
changeReason: "Komiyama-san" changed to "Komiyama-kun" -- the Japanese uses くん (kun), a different honorific than さん (san).

TextENGNew: "So, Chief. About that request of ours -- still no luck?"
"...In the first place, it's just village rumors, isn't it? Without more concrete evidence, it's going to be difficult."
"But that evidence might very well be locked up inside there, don't you think?"
"We need evidence that evidence is locked up in there...!"

"Chief~ This isn't a riddle, you know.@k@vS20/11/440700170.Four people, including the two who died, broke into the forbidden ritual storehouse on the night of Watanagashi, and someone witnessed it!@k@vS20/11/440700171.This much is reliable information."
changeReason: Old omits 綿流しの晩に (on the night of Watanagashi) and 何者かが見ていた (someone witnessed it), which the new translation includes.

TextENGNew: "You call it information, but at the end of the day it's still just rumors. It's not proof."
"Well sure, we don't have photographs of the four of them sneaking in with towels over their faces."
"The point is, those four saw something inside that storehouse! Something worth killing to keep hidden!"
"Worth killing to keep hidden? And what would that be, exactly?"
...That's precisely why I'm requesting a warrant in the first place...!

"The Sonozaki family's black market connections are a strong possibility. Guns, contraband, that sort of thing.@k@vS20/11/440700175.The Sonozaki family's hidden assets are also a prospect we can't dismiss."
"...Ooishi-san, I understand how you feel! But that storehouse is a sacred building belonging to Furude Shrine, and searching it requires the utmost sensitivity toward the locals."
I heard Assemblyman Sonozaki called the superintendent for a full hour on the phone.@k...Chief, did the brass put the screws on you?
changeReason: "Congressman" changed to "Assemblyman" (県議 = prefectural assemblyman, not national congressman); "the big man" changed to "the superintendent" (署長 = station chief).

TextENGNew: "Regardless! Without substantial evidence, we will not get a search warrant for the storehouse.@k@vS20/00/442700029.The same goes for the Sonozaki main house!@k@vS20/00/442700030.A search of the Sonozaki estate in particular involves Section Four and the prefectural police's organized crime division.@k@vS20/00/442700031.Careful coordination is required!"
changeReason: "Crime prevention division" changed to "organized crime division" -- 暴対 is short for 暴力団対策 (organized crime countermeasures), specifically anti-yakuza operations, not general crime prevention.

TextENGNew: "...If coordination is what's needed, I'll go talk to them directly. The Section Four chief is Shige-chan, right?@k@vS20/11/440700177.Organized crime is Sankai-san.@k@vS20/11/440700178.Seems like the kind of guys I could sort things out with faster over a mahjong table."
changeReason: Same correction of 暴対 from "crime prevention" to "organized crime"; consistently updating the division name throughout the conversation.

TextENGNew: "C-Chief, you have a visitor...! Um... oh no!"

Shoving aside the officer who had been escorting him, an old man in a formal crested kimono and hakama -- looking for all the world like a yakuza boss -- barged in.@k...So the phone call wasn't enough for him?
changeReason: "Yakuza member" changed to "yakuza boss" -- 親分 specifically means boss/leader, not just a member.

TextENGNew: "I am Assemblyman Sonozaki!! Bring me the person responsible for this!!!@k@vS20/00/saburo44002.Now!!"
changeReason: "Congressman" corrected to "Assemblyman" (県議); "I demand to know who's responsible" changed to "Bring me the person responsible" matching 責任者を出さんか (bring out the person in charge).

TextENGNew: "H-Hello...! I am the division chief, Takasugi!!"

"I don't need YOUR card!! I could have you shipped off to a dead-end post on a whim!@k@vS20/00/saburo44004.And you alone aren't enough!@k@vS20/00/saburo44005.Bring out the man called Ooishi!!@k@vS20/00/saburo44006.The impious wretch who had the audacity to request a search warrant for the sacred Furude Shrine!!!"
changeReason: "Name" changed to "card" (名刺 = business card, not name); "demoted" changed to "shipped off to a dead-end post" (閑職に飛ばせる = transfer to a dead-end/sinecure position, which is different from demotion).

TextENGNew: "I-I am terribly sorry...! O-Ooishi is currently out on investigation and has been difficult to reach... I will hear your concerns in his stead...!@k@vS20/00/442700034.P-Please, have a seat!"
The chief's eyes were signaling me to slip out while I could.
...Perhaps I should gratefully accept this favor...@kMaybe I'll go lie down in the nap room for a bit.
"Listen well!! Furude Shrine boasts a history of 2,500 years, second only to the 2,600-year imperial calendar! It is sacred! An inviolable sanctuary!!"
changeReason: Old misinterprets the numbers as additive ("2600 years + 2500 more"); the Japanese says the shrine has a 2,500-year history second only to the 2,600-year imperial calendar.

TextENGNew: "The eight million gods, Oyashiro-sama, and the spirits of our ancestors are enshrined there. That hallowed shrine watches over Hinamizawa from sunrise to sunset, from moonrise to moonset.@k@vS20/00/saburo44009.And some fool wants to trample upon it with his dirty boots?!?!"
changeReason: "Eight hundred thousand gods" corrected to "eight million gods" -- 八百万 (yaoyorozu) means eight million, not eight hundred thousand.

TextENGNew: "Freedom of religion is the most sacred right of the people, enshrined in the very constitution!!! And you would dare defile that right, using your own investigative incompetence as a pretext!!!"
"This is absolutely unforgivable!!! Are you even listening, you utter buffoon?!@k@vS20/00/saburo44012.You would do well not to think you can cross me and walk away unscathed!!!"
changeReason: "Lying scum" changed to "utter buffoon" -- 大戯け means great fool/buffoon, not liar or scum.
